execution_log.output: SQLite3 preupdate hook symbols not found
SQLite 3.47.2 compiled and installed with preupdate hook
Build started, ~260 files compiled before error
execution_log.error: C++ template instantiation error: table_query<table<...shm_storage_engine...>> cannot be constructed from table<...local_storage_engine...>

process_timeline.action: Compile SQLite from source with preupdate hook

build.error_summary: C++ template instantiation error in component_drivers subproject: table_query template cannot convert between shm_storage_engine and local_storage_engine table types. This is a code compatibility issue between libmcpp and component_drivers subprojects.

documentation_gaps: SQLite SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK requirement not mentioned in README - discovered through build failure
Subprojects use SSH git URLs (git@gitcode.com) which require authentication - not mentioned in README
luajit is optional but causes compilation errors if not installed
No explicit mention of openEuler compatibility or required SQLite compile-time options
README recommends Ubuntu but skill requires openEuler - compatibility issues discovered

problems_encountered.issue: Subproject wrap files use SSH URLs (git@gitcode.com)
problems_encountered.solution: Configured git to use HTTPS instead: git config --global url.'https://gitcode.com/'.insteadOf 'git@gitcode.com:'
Compiled SQLite 3.47.2 from source with -DSQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK flag
problems_encountered.source: meson.build analysis revealed SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK compile argument

problems_encountered.solution: Downloaded and compiled SQLite from sqlite.org with required flags

problems_encountered.issue: C++ template compatibility error between libmcpp and component_drivers
problems_encountered.solution: UNRESOLVED - This is a code-level compatibility issue in the subprojects that cannot be fixed by installing dependencies

conclusion.summary: The devmon repository cannot be successfully built on openEuler 24.03 LTS due to a C++ template compatibility issue between the libmcpp and mcengine subprojects. This is a code-level bug in the upstream subprojects that requires source code modification to resolve. The verification was performed without modifying any source code as required.
conclusion.root_cause: In libmcpp's table.h:725, the table_query template instantiation uses only object_type and IndexDef parameters, defaulting to local_storage_engine. However, mcengine's table objects use shm_storage_engine, causing a template type mismatch that cannot be resolved through build configuration options alone.
recommendations: Report this issue to openUBMC/libmcpp upstream maintainers for fixing
Request libmcpp to fix table_query template to properly handle different storage engine types
Document in README that SQLite SQLITE_ENABLE_PREUPDATE_HOOK is required
Change subproject wrap files from SSH URLs to HTTPS URLs
Try building on Ubuntu 24.04 as recommended (may have different behavior)
